description: Using an 8-node workflow, read data.csv, drop null values, remove duplicate rows, filter rows where salary is greater than 50000, select name, department, salary, sort by salary descending, group by department and count rows, then export to c_dept_counts.csv.

Using a 9-node workflow, read input.json, validate schema, apply a data transformation with no operations, drop null values, store into a SQLite database called c_events.db in table raw_events, run a SQL query to retrieve all rows, group by event_type and count rows, sort by count descending, then export to c_event_counts.csv.

Using an 8-node workflow, read data.csv, drop null values, remove duplicate rows, filter rows where score is greater than 0.4, cast column types using an empty mapping, group by label and count occurrences, sort by count descending, then export to c_label_counts.csv.

Using a 9-node workflow, read data.csv, drop null values, remove duplicate rows, select name, age, region, cast column types using an empty mapping, store into a SQLite database called c_people.db in table contacts, run a SQL query to retrieve all rows, sort by age ascending, then export to c_contacts.csv.

Using an 8-node workflow, read data.csv, filter rows where status equals active, remove duplicate rows, select name, status, region, cast column types using an empty mapping, store into a SQLite database called c_customers.db in table active_customers, run a SQL query to retrieve all rows, then export to c_active_customers.csv.

Using an 8-node workflow, read data.csv, drop null values, remove duplicate rows, filter rows where age is greater than 25, select name, age, salary, sort by age ascending, then export to c_people_age.csv.
